license.name: No public data license has been asserted yet

records.title: Albert Collins
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography of the Texas electric blues guitarist.

records.title: About Ally Venable
records.note: Official biography documenting Venable's Texas blues-rock career, guest recordings with Buddy Guy, Joe Bonamassa and Kingfish, and mentorship by Kenny Wayne Shepherd.

records.title: Willie Mae ‘Big Mama’ Thornton
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography of the singer, songwriter, harmonica player and drummer.

records.title: Billy Branch and the Sons of Blues Biography
records.note: Official biography documenting Branch's Chicago harmonica career and Willie Dixon's role in discovering and guiding him.

records.title: I Believe I’ll Go Back Home: Blindness in Blues and Gospel Race Records
records.note: University dissertation discussing later adaptations of Blind Willie Johnson's repertoire, including Led Zeppelin's recording of Nobody's Fault but Mine.

records.title: Johnson, ‘Blind Willie’
records.note: Handbook of Texas biography documenting Johnson's gospel-blues career, slide-guitar technique, Columbia sessions, and recorded repertoire.

records.title: Blues Hall of Fame Museum
records.note: Institutional page used only where an imported artist was explicitly named in the page content retrieved during the audit.

records.title: Brownie McGhee
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography of the Piedmont guitarist and longtime Sonny Terry collaborator.

records.title: History of Rhythm & Blues
records.note: Institutional genre history used only for imported artists explicitly named in the article.

records.title: About Cedric
records.note: Official biography documenting Burnside's Hill Country work and his teenage years drumming on the road with his grandfather R.L. Burnside.

records.title: Christone “Kingfish” Ingram
records.note: Municipal artist profile documenting Ingram's Clarksdale background and named influences, including Lightnin' Hopkins, Little Jimmy King, Eric Gales, and the three Kings.

records.title: D.K. Harrell
records.note: Label biography documenting Harrell's Louisiana background and close study of B.B. King's sound, technique, stagecraft and band leadership.

records.title: About Eric Clapton
records.note: The official biography dates Clapton's Yardbirds membership from October 1963 for an 18-month tenure.

records.title: How Eric Gales enlisted Buddy Guy, Kingfish and Joe Bonamassa to pay tribute to his brother
records.note: Direct interview documenting Little Jimmy King's formative instruction of his younger brother Eric Gales.

records.title: Eric Gales Biography
records.note: Label biography covering Gales's Memphis upbringing, upside-down guitar technique, and relationship to his older brother Little Jimmy King.

records.title: Fred McDowell
records.note: State heritage marker documenting McDowell's life, slide-guitar style, and formative importance to R.L. Burnside.

records.title: About Gare du Nord
records.note: Official history describing the group's 2001 launch, its jazz-and-blues concept, and Pablo's Blues as a track built around Robert Johnson's Come On in My Kitchen.

records.title: James Cotton
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography documenting Cotton's training with Sonny Boy Williamson II, recording with Howlin’ Wolf, and twelve years in Muddy Waters's band.

records.title: About Jesse Dayton
records.note: Official biography covering Dayton's Texas roots career and his Death Wish Blues collaboration with Samantha Fish.

records.title: Joe Bonamassa on the B.B. King Blues Summit 100
records.note: Direct interview in which Bonamassa identifies B.B. King as a mentor and describes their long relationship.

records.title: Biography
records.note: Official biography documenting Bonamassa's career and his opening performance for B.B. King at age twelve.

records.title: John Hammond
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography documenting Hammond's acoustic blues career and early inspiration from Jimmy Reed.

records.title: John Hammond (1942–2026)
records.note: Official site memorial describing Hammond's six-decade career in acoustic blues.

records.title: John Mayer
records.note: Institutional profile documenting Mayer's 1977 birth, work as a singer-songwriter and guitarist, blues study, and formative encounter with Stevie Ray Vaughan's music.

records.title: Johnny Copeland
records.note: Label biography of the Texas blues singer-guitarist, including Shemekia Copeland's teenage appearances on his tours.

records.title: Jontavious Willis Interview
records.note: Direct interview in which Willis describes Taj Mahal becoming a mentor and introducing him to Keb’ Mo’.

records.title: The Torch Burns Bright: A Visit with Jontavious Willis
records.note: Nonprofit profile describing Taj Mahal taking Willis under his wing and Keb’ Mo’ serving as another major mentor.

records.title: Electronic Press Kit
records.note: Official artist profile describing Willis's Georgia background, acoustic blues repertoire, and work with Taj Mahal and Keb’ Mo’.

records.title: Keb’ Mo’ reflects on his journey and Grammy nomination
records.note: Direct interview in which Keb’ Mo’ identifies Taj Mahal as his most important mentor.

records.title: Kenny Wayne Shepherd Band Bios
records.note: Official biography documenting Shepherd's 1977 North Louisiana birth and modern blues career.

records.title: Fresh off his Grammy win for 662, young bluesman Christone ‘Kingfish’ Ingram is just getting started
records.note: Recording Academy interview embedding the official music video for 662.

records.title: Christone ‘Kingfish’ Ingram has learned from the masters on his road to becoming a modern-day blues great
records.note: Direct interview in which Ingram explains how Eric Gales expanded his blues-rock vocabulary.

records.title: Biography
records.note: Official biography describing Ingram's Clarksdale roots, modern blues fusion, and guitar vocabulary shaped by B.B. King, Albert King, and Buddy Guy.

records.title: Koko Taylor
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography identifying Bessie Smith and Big Mama Thornton among Taylor's strongest vocal influences.

records.title: Lightnin’ Hopkins
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography of the influential Texas country blues singer-guitarist.

records.title: Little Jimmy King Biography
records.note: Reference biography of the Memphis-based left-handed guitarist and his recordings with Eric and Eugene Gales.

records.title: Blues
records.note: The Library of Congress overview identifies Son House as having taught Muddy Waters.

records.title: R.L. Burnside
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography documenting Burnside's North Mississippi Hill Country career and Fred McDowell's influence.

records.title: Robert Cray
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography documenting Cray's career and the Showdown! album with Albert Collins and Johnny Copeland.

records.title: About Samantha Fish
records.note: Official biography covering Fish's contemporary blues career and the Death Wish Blues album with Jesse Dayton.

records.title: History of Blues Music
records.note: Educational overview used only for artists explicitly named in its regional and historical sections.

records.title: Shemekia Copeland speaks out on new Done Come Too Far album
records.note: Direct interview in which Copeland describes touring with and learning from her father Johnny Copeland.

records.title: Biography
records.note: Official biography covering Copeland's Harlem upbringing and early stage appearances with her father Johnny Copeland.

records.title: Sippie Wallace
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography documenting Victoria Spivey's encouragement of Wallace's return and Bonnie Raitt's later role in renewing attention to her work.

records.title: Sister Rosetta Tharpe: Gospel’s first electric guitar star
records.note: Smithsonian collection essay documenting Tharpe's electric-guitar legacy and influence on Chuck Berry and Little Richard.

records.title: Skip James
records.note: Scholarly state encyclopedia entry documenting James's Bentonia style, 1960s rediscovery, and Cream's later recording of I'm So Glad.

records.title: Before the Blues: From Africa to the United States
records.note: Supports a broad historical connection between West African traditions and field hollers, but not a direct unbroken lineage or the record's specific timbral comparison.

records.title: Sonny Terry
records.note: Blues Hall of Fame biography documenting Terry's long recording partnership with Brownie McGhee and sessions with Lightnin’ Hopkins.

records.title: TajMo
records.note: Official project page and artist biographies documenting Taj Mahal and Keb’ Mo’s recorded collaboration.

records.title: Blues Hall of Fame 2020 inductees named
records.note: Blues Foundation biography summary of singer, pianist, songwriter, bandleader and label owner Victoria Spivey.

records.title: List of blues musicians
records.note: Discovery and blues-classification roster containing 462 table entries when audited. It does not establish biographies, dates, or relationships; imported candidates require an individual profile source.
